prerequisites: An active Brevo account (Brevo is the current name for the platform formerly called Sendinblue).
A Brevo API key generated from your account, which the connector requires as the `api_key` config field.
Access to the Brevo API endpoints over HTTPS at https://api.brevo.com (the v3 REST API).

authMethods.name: API key (api-key header)
authMethods.summary: The connector authenticates with a single Brevo API key, supplied as the required secret `api_key` config field. Brevo authenticates each request by reading the key from the `api-key` HTTP header. Generate the key in your Brevo account's API keys settings and keep it secret.

setupSteps.title: Create or sign in to a Brevo account
setupSteps.body: Brevo is the rebranded name of Sendinblue, so existing Sendinblue credentials carry over. Sign in to (or create) your Brevo account before generating a key.

setupSteps.title: Generate an API key
setupSteps.body: Open your Brevo API keys settings, click 'Generate a new API key', name it, and save the value somewhere secure. Brevo only shows the full key once, so copy it immediately. This key is what the API authenticates against via the `api-key` header.

setupSteps.title: Configure the connector
setupSteps.body: Enter the generated key into the connector's required `api_key` field. This is the only configuration value the connector needs; it is stored as a secret.

setupSteps.title: Mind Brevo's rate limits
setupSteps.body: Brevo enforces per-endpoint rate limits (for example, contact endpoints allow about 10 requests/second and 36,000 requests/hour, while most other endpoints are limited to 100 requests/hour). Exceeding a limit returns HTTP 429 Too Many Requests, so plan extract frequency accordingly.

sources.title: Brevo API Documentation: Getting started quickstart (API key creation)
